claim: This text contains no substantive scientific claims; it consists solely of author affiliations and contact information.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
section_heading: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products: A Computational Approach to Targeting AgeRelated Glycative Damage (Stage 1 Registered Report)
rationale: The text contains a detailed abstract outlining a specific research project, including hypotheses, methodologies, and expected outcomes regarding de novo protein binder design for AGEs. This constitutes substantive scientific claims.

claim: The top 100 -200 computational designs will be validated experimentally through yeast surface display screening against CML-modified bovine serum albumin, and top candidates will undergo binding affinity characterization by biolayer interferometry.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
rationale: The claim is directly supported by the Abstract, which explicitly states the plan to validate top computational designs via yeast surface display and characterize affinity using biolayer interferometry.

claim: We hypothesize that this pipeline will yield at least one binder with measurable binding to CML-modified protein and sub-micromolar affinity.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
rationale: The abstract explicitly states the hypothesis that the pipeline will yield at least one binder with measurable binding to CML-modified protein and sub-micromolar affinity.

claim: This work represents the first application of RFdiffusion All-Atom to AGE targeting and will establish whether computationally designed protein binders can serve as a new class of anti-glycation therapeutics.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
rationale: The abstract explicitly states the project applies deep learning-based protein design to create de novo binders for CML (an AGE). It further confirms this is the first application of RFdiffusion All-Atom to AGE targeting and aims to establish if these binders can serve as new anti-glycation therapeutics.

claim: AGE cross-links increase tissue stiffness and reduce turnover in long-lived extracellular matrix proteins like collagen.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
section_heading: Research Question: Background, Importance, and Relevance
rationale: The text explicitly states that AGE cross-links increase tissue stiffness and reduce turnover in long-lived extracellular matrix proteins like collagen.

claim: None have achieved regulatory approval for AGE-related indications.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
section_heading: Research Question: Background, Importance, and Relevance
rationale: The text explicitly states that current therapeutic approaches for AGEs, including inhibitors, breakers, and antagonists, have not achieved regulatory approval for AGE-related indications.

claim: Aminoguanidine reached Phase III trials but was discontinued due to adverse effects.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
section_heading: Research Question: Background, Importance, and Relevance
rationale: The text explicitly states that aminoguanidine reached Phase III trials but was discontinued due to adverse effects.

claim: No group has yet applied this technology to AGE targeting.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
section_heading: Research Question: Background, Importance, and Relevance
rationale: The text explicitly states that while the technology has been validated for other small molecule targets, no group has yet applied it to AGE targeting.

claim: This project proposes a novel strategy: designing de novo protein binders that recognize and sequester specific AGE adducts.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
section_heading: Research Question: Background, Importance, and Relevance
rationale: The text explicitly states the project proposes a novel strategy to design de novo protein binders that recognize and sequester specific AGE adducts, directly matching the claim.

claim: Unlike small molecules, designed proteins can achieve high affinity and specificity through extensive complementary surfaces.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
section_heading: Research Question: Background, Importance, and Relevance
rationale: The text explicitly states this claim as the rationale for the proposed strategy, contrasting designed proteins with small molecules regarding affinity and specificity.

claim: This approach has been validated for other small molecule targets including digoxigenin and heme.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
section_heading: Research Question: Background, Importance, and Relevance
rationale: The text explicitly states that the RFdiffusion All-Atom approach has been validated for other small molecule targets, specifically listing digoxigenin and heme.

claim: A designed AGE binder could function therapeutically by intercepting circulating AGEs before they engage RAGE or cross-link tissue proteins.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
section_heading: Research Question: Background, Importance, and Relevance
rationale: The text explicitly states this mechanism as a potential therapeutic function of the proposed designed AGE binders, describing how they would intercept circulating AGEs to prevent RAGE engagement and tissue cross-linking.

claim: Such binders would serve as research tools for detecting and quantifying specific AGE species, and as scaffolds for downstream enzyme design to actively degrade AGEs.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
section_heading: Research Question: Background, Importance, and Relevance
rationale: The text explicitly states that designed AGE binders would serve as research tools for detecting and quantifying specific AGE species and as scaffolds for downstream enzyme design to actively degrade AGEs.

claim: This study will test three preregistered hypotheses: H1: RFdiffusion All-Atom can generate protein scaffolds with designed binding pockets for CML (the most abundant AGE species).
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
rationale: The Hypotheses section explicitly states the study will test three preregistered hypotheses, with H1 defined as testing if RFdiffusion All-Atom can generate protein scaffolds with designed binding pockets for CML.

claim: H2: At least one computationally designed binder will show measurable binding to CMLmodified bovine serum albumin (AGE-BSA) in yeast surface display screening, defined as a signal-to-background ratio greater than 3.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
rationale: The claim is explicitly stated as Hypothesis H2 in the provided text. The document defines the specific criteria for this hypothesis, including the target (CML-modified BSA), the method (yeast surface display), and the quantitative threshold (signal-to-background ratio > 3).

claim: H3: Top binder candidates will exhibit sub-micromolar affinity for AGE-BSA as measured by biolayer interferometry (BLI).
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
rationale: The text explicitly lists H3 as a preregistered hypothesis stating that top binders will exhibit sub-micromolar affinity for AGE-BSA measured by BLI. The timeline confirms BLI is the planned method for affinity characterization.

claim: H1 is the primary outcome as it establishes the foundational feasibility of the computational approach.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
rationale: The text explicitly states that H1 is the primary outcome because it establishes the foundational feasibility of the computational approach.

claim: H2 constitutes the primary experimental outcome, since yeast display screening is the initial gate for identifying active binders.

claim: H3 is a secondary outcome that characterizes the quality of candidates passing the primary screen.

claim: The Methods section provides sufficient detail to allow full replication of all computational and experimental procedures.

rationale: The provided text explicitly states that the Methods section contains sufficient detail for full replication of all procedures, directly matching the claim.

claim: Carboxymethyllysine (CML) will serve as the primary design target.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
rationale: The Methods section explicitly states that Carboxymethyllysine (CML) will serve as the primary design target due to its abundance and stability.

claim: CML is the most abundant AGE in human tissues, is chemically stable, has commercially available standards, and its structure is well characterized.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
rationale: The Methods section explicitly states that CML is the most abundant AGE in human tissues, chemically stable, has commercially available standards, and its structure is well characterized as the basis for target selection.

claim: The molecular structure of CML bound to a lysine backbone will be used as the design input.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
rationale: The Methods section explicitly states that the molecular structure of CML bound to a lysine backbone will be used as the design input for generating coordinates.

claim: The study design follows a three-stage workflow: backbone generation using RFdiffusion All-Atom, sequence design using ProteinMPNN, and in silico filtering using AlphaFold2 and Rosetta to advance top designs to experimental testing.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
rationale: The text explicitly outlines a three-stage computational design pipeline: Stage 1 uses RFdiffusion All-Atom for backbone generation, Stage 2 uses ProteinMPNN for sequence design, and Stage 3 uses AlphaFold2 and Rosetta for in silico filtering to select top designs for testing.

claim: A total of 10,000 backbone designs will be generated conditioned on the CML structure to produce diverse binding pocket geometries.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
rationale: The 'Computational Design Pipeline' section explicitly states that Stage 1 involves generating 10,000 backbone designs conditioned on the CML structure to produce diverse binding pocket geometries.

claim: Five independent sequences will be generated per backbone, optimized for the predicted binding interface.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
rationale: The 'Computational Design Pipeline' section explicitly states that 'ProteinMPNN will assign amino acid sequences... Five independent sequences will be generated per backbone.' This directly supports the claim.

claim: Designs will be filtered based on structural confidence (pLDDT > 85) and Rosetta binding energy estimation, with the top 100-200 designs advancing to experimental testing.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
rationale: The claim is directly supported by the 'In silico filtering' section, which explicitly states designs will be scored using AlphaFold2 (pLDDT) and Rosetta, with top 100-200 designs advancing to testing.

claim: Designed binders will be displayed on yeast cell surfaces as Aga2p fusions.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
rationale: The 'Yeast Surface Display Screening' section explicitly states that designed binders will be displayed on yeast cell surfaces as Aga2p fusions, directly confirming the claim.

claim: A pooled library of top designs will be constructed through oligonucleotide synthesis and Gibson assembly.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
rationale: The 'Yeast Surface Display Screening' section explicitly states that a pooled library of top designs will be constructed through oligonucleotide synthesis and Gibson assembly.

claim: The library will be sorted against biotinylated AGE-BSA using fluorescence-activated cell sorting (FACS).
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
rationale: The Yeast Surface Display Screening section explicitly states that the library will be sorted against biotinylated AGE-BSA using fluorescence-activated cell sorting (FACS).

claim: Binding cells will be enriched over 2 -3 rounds of selection.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
rationale: The Yeast Surface Display Screening section explicitly states that binding cells will be enriched over 2-3 rounds of selection using FACS against biotinylated AGE-BSA.

claim: Individual clones from the enriched population will be sequenced by Sanger sequencing and characterized.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
rationale: The Yeast Surface Display Screening section explicitly states that individual clones from the enriched population will be sequenced by Sanger sequencing and characterized.

claim: Binding kinetics (kon, koff) and equilibrium affinity (KD) will be measured using biolayer interferometry (BLI) on an Octet RED96 instrument (Sartorius) with AGE-BSA immobilized on streptavidin biosensors.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
rationale: The 'Binding Characterization' section explicitly states that binding kinetics (kon, koff) and equilibrium affinity (KD) will be measured using biolayer interferometry (BLI) on an Octet RED96 instrument with AGE-BSA immobilized on streptavidin biosensors.

claim: Specificity will be assessed by parallel testing against unmodified BSA at equivalent concentrations.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
rationale: The Binding Characterization section explicitly states that specificity will be assessed by parallel testing against unmodified BSA at equivalent concentrations, directly matching the claim.

claim: No exclusion criteria apply to the computational designs; all designs passing the pre-specified in silico filtering thresholds will advance to experimental testing.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
rationale: The 'Blinding and Bias Minimization' section explicitly states that no exclusion criteria apply to computational designs and that all designs passing pre-specified in silico thresholds advance to testing.

claim: This estimate is conservative given that AGE-BSA presents multiple CML epitopes per molecule, increasing the effective target valency and concentration relative to single-epitope targets used in prior benchmarks.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
rationale: The 'Sample and Statistical Power' section explicitly states that the estimate is conservative because AGE-BSA has multiple CML epitopes, increasing effective target valency compared to single-epitope benchmarks.

claim: The minimum detectable effect is defined operationally: a hit rate of ≥ 1% (at least 1 binder out of 100 tested) would be considered evidence that the computational pipeline can identify functional binders, consistent with first-in-class platform development.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
rationale: The claim is directly supported by the 'Sample and Statistical Power' section, which explicitly defines the minimum detectable effect as a hit rate of ≥1% for the primary outcome.

claim: Data will be collected from four sources: (i) RFdiffusion All-Atom output files (PDB format) for backbone designs; (ii) AlphaFold2 pLDDT scores and predicted aligned error (PAE) matrices; (iii) Rosetta binding energy scores; and (iv) BLI sensorgrams from the Octet RED96.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: FACS data will be collected on a BD FACSAria instrument and analyzed using FlowJo (v10).

claim: Computational designs will be filtered by the following sequential pre-processing criteria prior to experimental testing: (1) pLDDT > 85 across the full design; (2) predicted TM-score to design model > 0.8; (3) no steric clashes with CML ligand as assessed by Rosetta fa_rep term < 5.0. Designs failing any criterion are excluded from experimental testing.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
rationale: The claim is directly supported by the 'Pre-Processing' section, which explicitly lists the exact sequential filtering criteria (pLDDT, TM-score, steric clashes) and the exclusion rule for designs failing these tests.

claim: BLI sensorgrams will be double-referenced (reference channel and buffer-only reference) prior to kinetic fitting using the Octet Analysis Studio software (Sartorius, v12).
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
rationale: The Pre-Processing section explicitly states that BLI sensorgrams will be double-referenced using a reference channel and buffer-only reference prior to kinetic fitting with Octet Analysis Studio v12.

claim: The primary outcome is the fraction of designs showing a binding signal above background in yeast surface display (hit rate).
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: Designs with a signal-to-background ratio > 3 relative to noligand controls will be classified as hits.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
rationale: The Primary Analysis section explicitly states that designs with a signal-to-background ratio > 3 relative to noligand controls will be classified as hits.

claim: The hit rate and its 95% Wilson confidence interval will be reported.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc

claim: Structural diversity of successful binders, assessed by pairwise backbone RMSD clustering.

rationale: The Secondary Analyses section explicitly states that structural diversity of successful binders will be assessed using pairwise backbone RMSD clustering.

claim: No imputation will be applied to missing BLI measurements; any binder for which a reliable fit cannot be obtained will be reported as ' fit failed ' with the raw data provided.

rationale: The 'Variations from Intended Sample Size' section explicitly states that no imputation will be applied to missing BLI measurements and that failed fits will be reported with raw data.

claim: AlphaFold2 predicts high confidence (pLDDT > 85) for 15 -20% of designs after ProteinMPNN sequence assignment, consistent with published benchmarks for the method.

rationale: The Pilot Data section explicitly states that AlphaFold2 predicts high confidence (pLDDT > 85) for 15-20% of designs after ProteinMPNN sequence assignment, matching the claim exactly.

claim: If no designs show a signal-to-background ratio > 3 in the initial yeast display screen, then the following pre-specified contingent analyses will be executed: testing binding to free CMLlysine conjugate to determine whether the absence of binding reflects a scaffold problem or a target accessibility problem, and assessing whether designs adopt the computationally predicted fold using circular dichroism spectroscopy to determine whether folding failures explain the absence of binding.
doc_name: De Novo Protein Binder Design for Advanced Glycation End Products A Computational Approach to Targeting Age-Related Glyc
rationale: The 'Contingent Analysis' section explicitly states that if no designs show a signal-to-background ratio > 3, the study will execute two specific analyses: testing binding to free CMLlysine conjugate and assessing fold via CD spectroscopy. This matches the claim exactly.

claim: If H2 and H3 are supported (binding detected with sub-micromolar affinity), this will establish proof-of-concept for a new class of AGE-targeting protein therapeutics and produce binder sequences that can be optimized through directed evolution.

rationale: The [Interpreting Results] section explicitly states that if H2 and H3 are supported with sub-micromolar affinity, it will establish proof-of-concept for a new class of AGE-targeting protein therapeutics and produce binder sequences for directed evolution.

claim: This would represent the first demonstration that computationally designed proteins can specifically engage a posttranslational glycation modification, advancing both the therapeutic and research tool landscape for aging biology.

rationale: The 'Interpreting Results' section explicitly states that if hypotheses H2 and H3 are supported, this outcome 'would represent the first demonstration' of computationally designed proteins engaging posttranslational glycation modifications, directly matching the claim.

rationale: The 'Interpreting Results' section explicitly states that if binding is not achieved, negative results will inform the field about design challenges and guide future computational approaches.
